Name: Clay
Effect: On enter: If you have a human, draw a human.
Anecdote: "Aruru washed her hands, she pinched off clay and cast it on the steppe. On the steppe she created Enkidu to rival Gilgamesh's power."

Name: Ninsun, Mother of Gilgamesh
Effect: On enter: Search your deck for Gilgamesh and add him to your hand. If he is already in play, move him to this location instead.
Anecdote: "O Ninsun, you are wise and know everything... Intercede for me with Shamash the Sun-God, for my path is long."

Name: Alewife Siduri
Effect: On enter: You may move one of your beings here to another location.
Anecdote: "'Gilgamesh, where are you hurrying? You will never find that life for which you looking.'"

Name: Trapper
Effect: On enter: Draw Enkidu
Anecdote: "A hunter, a trapper, faced Enkidu at the watering hole... 'Father, a man has come from the hills, he fills up my pits and breaks my traps.'"

Name: Gilgamesh
Effect: Gilgamesh's power is equal to 1 + the power of all monsters in your underworld. While Enkidu is at the same location as Gilgamesh, they are immortal
Anecdote: "He built the walls of Uruk, the sacred storehouse. Two-thirds of him is god, and one-third is human."

Name: Enkidu
Effect: While on top: You may move Enkidu to Gilgamesh. Enkidu's Power is equal to Gilgamesh's.
Anecdote: "With the gazelles he eats wild grass, with the beasts he jostles at the waterhole. He knew neither people nor a homeland."

Name: Shamhat
Effect: On enter: Play Enkidu here from your hand, deck, or underworld.
Anecdote: "Shamhat bared her bosom, and he possessed her ripeness... For six days and seven nights Enkidu stayed erect, and forgot the wild where he was born."

Name: Ferryman Urshanabi
Effect: On enter: You may move one of your heroes to here.
Anecdote: "'He is the one who crosses the Waters of Death, to reach Utnapishtim the Faraway.'"

Name: Mountain Lions
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have one or more heroes here. Reward: Draw a card and move a hero from here to another location.
Anecdote: "'When I arrived at the mountain passes at night, I saw lions and grew terrified. I raised my axe and struck them down.'"

Name: Scorpion-Men
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have one or more heroes here. Reward: Draw two cards.
Anecdote: "At the mountains of Mashu, Scorpio-men guard the gate... whose glance is death, whose radiant halo sweeps the peaks."

Name: The Serpent
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have one or more heroes here. Reward: Each opponent must discard a card.
Anecdote: "A serpent caught the scent of the plant of youth; it came up silently from the pool and snatched it away, instantly shedding its old skin."

Name: Ishtar
Effect: While on top: Whenever one of your heroes moves away from here, each opponent must banish a creature with a cost of [2] or lower.
Anecdote: "Princess Ishtar raised her eyes to his beauty: 'Come, Gilgamesh, be my husband! But he replied: Which of your lovers did you ever love forever?'"

Name: Utnapishtim, Survivor of the Flood
Effect: Immortal.
Anecdote: "'Man of Shuruppak, build a great boat! Abandon wealth and look for life... For the gods have given me eternal life among the stars.'"

Name: Humbaba, Guardian of the Cedar Forest
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have two or more heroes here. Reward: You may play a card of cost [5] or less for free.
Anecdote: "His roar is the Flood, his mouth is Fire, his breath is Death. Enlil appointed him to guard the Cedar Forest to terrify mortals."

Name: Bull of Heaven
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have two or more heroes here. Reward: Each opponent must banish two of their beings.
Anecdote: "Ishtar cried to her father: 'Create the Bull of Heaven to strike Gilgamesh down!' With its first snort, a chasm opened and swallowed a hundred men."

Name: Šara, Inanna's Beautician
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Inanna, Goddess of Love and War' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'Šara is my singer, my manicurist and my hairdresser. How could I turn him over to you?'

Name: Kur-Jara
Effect: On enter: If 'Gala-Tura' is at this location, revive a being with a cost of [3] or less.
Anecdote: He removed some dirt from the tip of his fingernail and created the kur-jara. To the kur-jara he gave the life-giving plant.

Name: Gala-Tura
Effect: On enter: If 'Kur-Jara' is at this location, revive a being with a cost of [3] or less.
Anecdote: He removed some dirt from the tip of his other fingernail and created the gala-tura. To the gala-tura he gave the life-giving water.

Name: Gatekeeper Neti
Effect: On enter: You may put a being from your hand into your underworld.
Anecdote: 'Be satisfied, Inanna, a divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled. You must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld.'

Name: Ninšubur, Sukkal to Inanna
Effect: On enter: If 'Inanna, Goddess of Love and War' is in your underworld, you may banish a friendly being to revive her.
Anecdote: She did not forget her orders, she did not neglect her instructions. Like a pauper, she clothed herself in a single garment.

Name: Galla Demons
Effect: On enter: You must banish one of your other beings if possible.
Anecdote: Those who accompanied her know no food, know no drink. They accept no pleasant gifts. They never enjoy the pleasures of the marital embrace.

Name: Geshtinanna, Dumuzid's Sister
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may banish this being to revive 'Dumuzid, Shepherd God'. On Revive: Draw a card.
Anecdote: 'You for half the year and your sister for half the year: when you are demanded, on that day you will stay, when your sister is demanded, on that day you will be released.'

Name: Sirtur, Mourning Mother
Effect: On enter: Revive 'Dumuzid, Shepherd God' or 'Geshtinanna, Dumuzid's Sister'.
Anecdote: Mother of Dumuzid, she mourned at the edge of the steppe, weeping for her shepherd son.

Name: Dirt under Enki's Fingernail
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Kur-Jara' and 'Gala-Tura' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'What has my daughter done? She has me worried. What has the mistress of all the lands done? She has me worried.'

Name: Underworld Courier
Effect: On enter: Put a being from your hand into your underworld.
Anecdote: Vizier of the Queen of the Dead, whose touch brings plague and whose word is fate.

Name: Inanna, Goddess of Love and War
Effect: On revive: For every opponent, banish one of their beings if possible.
Anecdote: From the great heaven she set her mind on the great below. My mistress abandoned heaven, abandoned earth, and descended to the underworld.

Name: Dumuzid, Shepherd God
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may banish this being to revive 'Geshtinanna, Dumuzid's Sister'.
Anecdote: There was Dumuzid clothed in a magnificent garment and seated magnificently on a throne. She looked at him — it was the look of death.

Name: Lulal, Inanna's Bodyguard
Effect: On enter: Revive 'Inanna, Goddess of Love and War'.
Anecdote: 'Outstanding Lulal follows me at my right and my left. How could I turn him over to you?'

Name: Namtar, Sukkal to Ereshkigal
Effect: On enter: Put any being from your hand, deck or battlefield into your underworld.
Anecdote: Her holy shoulders were not covered by a linen cloth. Her nails were like a pickaxe upon her. The hair on her head was bunched up as if it were leeks.

Name: Anunnaki, The Seven Judges
Effect: While on top: Whenever you revive a being, each opponent must banish one of their beings.
Anecdote: They looked at her — it was the look of death. They spoke to her — it was the speech of anger.
